faqPills.label: How is this different from other herb books?
faqPills.answer: Most herb books organize around what an herb does. This one organizes around what you need to know before you use it — dose, interactions, safety flags, confidence level. If your current books include milligram dosing ranges by form and age group, you may not need this. If they don't, this is the one that fills that gap.

faqPills.label: Is this evidence-based?
faqPills.answer: Every herb profile includes a three-tier confidence rating: Traditional Use, Some Evidence, or Stronger Evidence. Where clinical studies exist, the handbook references them. Where only traditional use exists, it says so plainly. No overpromising.

faqPills.label: Does it cover drug interactions?
faqPills.answer: Yes. The handbook flags documented interactions with common medication categories — SSRIs, blood thinners, birth control, blood pressure medications, and more — directly on each herb profile.

problem.title: Most Herb Guides Tell You Everything Except the One Thing You Need
paragraphs: You've looked up an herb and found three different answers on three different sites. You've held a bottle of elderberry and realized you had no idea whether the dose was right for your nine-year-old. You've read an herb book cover to cover and walked away knowing what chamomile is used for — but not how much to actually take.

Most herbal guides tell you everything except the one thing you need at the moment you're standing in your kitchen making a decision: the dose. The interaction. The safety note that should have been on the first page.
That's the gap. And it's the reason this handbook exists.
problem.emphasisLine: You already know the problem. This is the reference that finally solves it.

image.alt: A kitchen counter with three different herb books open to the same herb, each showing different and incomplete dosing information — illustrating the inconsistency problem

solution.title: What Makes This Handbook Structurally Different
paragraphs: Most herb guides are organized around what an herb does. This one is organized around what you need to know before you use it.

Every herb profile in The Honest Herbalist Handbook starts with three things most references skip: dosing ranges by form and by age group, interaction flags for common medications, and a confidence rating for every listed use.
solution.emphasisLine: This isn't a book you read once and shelve. It's a reference you open when you're holding a bottle and need an answer.

bullets.title: Dosing ranges — by form and by age group
bullets.body: Actual milligram ranges for tinctures, capsules, teas, and glycerites. Adult ranges and pediatric ranges, clearly separated. When a dose is based on published clinical data, it says so. When it's based on traditional use, it says that too.

bullets.title: Interaction flags for common medications
bullets.body: SSRIs. Blood thinners. Birth control. Blood pressure meds. If an herb has a documented interaction with a medication that millions of people take daily, that flag is on the profile — not buried in an appendix.

bullets.title: A confidence rating for every listed use
bullets.body: A three-tier system — Traditional Use, Some Evidence, Stronger Evidence — so you know the difference between centuries of folk use and a controlled clinical study.

image.alt: A clear, well-organized herb profile page from The Honest Herbalist Handbook showing the three structural pillars: dosing columns, interaction flags highlighted in amber, and the confidence rating badge

callout.leftTitle: Built for the Way You Actually Use Herbs
callout.leftBody: You're not trying to become a clinical herbalist. You're the person who reaches for chamomile before melatonin, who gives your kid honey and thyme before calling the pediatrician — but who also wants to know you're not accidentally making things worse. Natural-first, but not reckless.
callout.rightBody: You don't need a certification. You need a reference that respects the way you already think about your family's health. This handbook was built for that exact mindset — curious, but careful.

guarantee.title: The "Look It Up" Guarantee
paragraphs: When your handbook arrives, open it to any herb you already use. Check the dosing range. Check the interaction flags. Check the confidence rating.
If the information isn't more specific, more organized, and more useful than what you've been relying on — return it. Full refund. No questionnaire. No hoops.
guarantee.whyBody: We're confident because the book is built to pass that test on the first page you open. Every herb profile leads with the information other guides leave out. One look and you'll feel the difference.
guarantee.closingLine: 30 days. Any herb. If it doesn't change how you make decisions, send it back.

image.alt: The Honest Herbalist Handbook lying open on a kitchen counter with a cup of herbal tea beside it — showing a real-use scenario that reflects the guarantee's 'look it up' test

items.question: I already have several herb books. How is this different?
items.answer: Most herb books organize around what an herb does. This one organizes around what you need to know before you use it — dose, interactions, safety flags, confidence level. If your current books include milligram dosing ranges by form and age group, you may not need this. If they don't, this is the one that fills that gap.

items.question: Is this evidence-based?
items.answer: Every herb profile includes a three-tier confidence rating: Traditional Use, Some Evidence, or Stronger Evidence. Where clinical studies exist, the handbook references them. Where only traditional use exists, it says so plainly. No overpromising.

items.question: I'm on medications. Does this cover drug interactions?
items.answer: Yes. The handbook flags documented interactions with common medication categories — SSRIs, blood thinners, birth control, blood pressure medications, and more — directly on each herb profile.

items.question: Is this safe to use for children's dosing?
items.answer: The handbook includes pediatric dosing ranges with age-based cutoffs. It also flags herbs that are not appropriate for children and notes when professional guidance is recommended.

items.question: What if I'm not satisfied?
items.answer: Return it within 30 days for a full refund. No questionnaire. Open the handbook to any herb you already use — if the information isn't more specific and more useful than what you've been relying on, send it back.

whyBundle.title: Why the Handbook Includes Digital Bonus Guides
whyBundle.body: Your physical handbook ships to your door, but you shouldn't have to wait to start making safer decisions. The two digital bonus guides give you instant access to the most critical safety information and a practical starter path — so you can begin using what you learn today, not next week.

quotes.text: I printed the Quick-Check Guide and stuck it on the fridge the same day I ordered. By the time the handbook arrived, I'd already caught an interaction I didn't know about.
quotes.author: Sarah M.

quotes.text: The Starter Guide helped me build a basic apothecary while I waited for the handbook to ship. I felt like I was already getting value before the box arrived.
quotes.author: Amanda P.

freeGifts.title: Your 2 Free Digital Bonus Guides
freeGifts.body:
Bonus 1: Herbal Safety Quick-Check Guide — A printable one-page reference covering the most common herb-drug interactions, pregnancy and nursing flags, and pediatric cautions. Stick it on the fridge. Use it today.

Bonus 2: Home Apothecary Starter Guide — A practical walkthrough of the 12 herbs worth having in your cabinet, how to source them, what to make first, and a budget-conscious shopping list. Start building your apothecary while the handbook ships.

Both guides are delivered digitally — instant access the moment you order.
